1:
A sold-out **Estadio Azteca** witnessed the perfect send-off for the hosts on Thursday, as **Mexico** closed out the group stage with a commanding 2-0 victory over **South Africa** to finish atop **Group A** with a flawless nine points from three wins. Playing in front of their passionate home crowd in Mexico City, El Tri were dominant throughout, sealing their place in the Round of 32 in emphatic fashion and sending a message to the rest of the field.

For **South Africa**, the result stings but doesn't end their tournament hopes — just yet. Bafana Bafana finish second in the group on four points, a record of one win, one draw, and one loss, and a goal difference of -1. That's enough to earn an automatic berth in the Round of 32 as one of the top two finishers in the group, meaning they'll move on alongside the hosts.

**Korea Republic** and **Czechia**, meanwhile, are left waiting anxiously. The Koreans sit third on three points with a goal difference of -1, while **Czechia** prop up the group with just a single point and a -4 goal difference. Whether either team advances depends entirely on how they stack up against the other third-placed sides from around the tournament — only the best eight third-place finishers earn a spot in the Round of 32. Both sides will be glued to results elsewhere as the group stage wraps up across all venues.
2:
A must-win match for both sides turned into a gripping afternoon at **Estadio Akron** in Guadalajara, as **Korea Republic** edged past **Czechia** 2-1 to secure three crucial points in **Group A** and keep their hopes of advancing very much alive.

With **Mexico** having swept through the group with a perfect nine points and **South Africa** sitting on four points ahead of kickoff, the math was unforgiving for both teams. **Korea Republic** needed the win badly, and they delivered, claiming their first victory of the group stage after back-to-back defeats. **Czechia**, meanwhile, suffered their second loss in three matches, leaving them with just a single point and on the verge of an early exit.

The result sees **Korea Republic** finish third in **Group A** on three points with a goal difference of -1, meaning their fate is no longer in their own hands. They must now wait and see whether their record is good enough to be among the eight best third-placed teams from across the tournament — a tense watch that will play out over the coming days. **South Africa** claim second place on four points and join **Mexico** in advancing automatically to the Round of 32. **Czechia** finish bottom of the group on one point and are eliminated from Football 2026, their tournament ending at the group stage.
3:
A tense afternoon at **BMO Field** in Toronto ended with both sides sharing the spoils, as **Canada** and **Bosnia and Herzegovina** played out a 1-1 draw to close out Group B — a result that left the hosts breathing easy but only just. With **Switzerland** already through as group winners on 7 points, it was the second automatic berth that was up for grabs, and **Canada** held on to it, finishing level on 4 points with **Bosnia and Herzegovina** but edging them out on goal difference in what turned out to be a nail-biting final reckoning.

The draw means **Canada**, in their first appearance at this level in 36 years, advance to the Round of 32 as group runners-up — a moment that will resonate deeply with a home crowd that packed BMO Field hoping for exactly this outcome. **Bosnia and Herzegovina**, also finishing on 4 points, drop to third in Group B with a goal difference of -1, and their fate now rests in the hands of the broader standings: they will need to rank among the eight best third-placed teams across all groups to survive into the knockout rounds.

**Qatar**, meanwhile, finish bottom of the group on just 1 point with a goal difference of -8, their tournament over. For the Canadians, the relief of qualifying on home soil will give way quickly to preparation for their Round of 32 fixture — details on their opponent, timing, and venue still to be confirmed once all group stage results are in. For **Bosnia and Herzegovina**, the wait begins.
4:
A raucous home crowd at SoFi Stadium in Inglewood got exactly what they came for, as **USA** dismantled **Paraguay** 4-1 to finish top of **Group D** and punch their ticket to the Round of 32. The result ends any suspense in the group standings, with the Americans claiming six points from their three matches and posting a goal difference of +4 to seal first place in convincing fashion.

The hosts were in control from early on, and the margin of victory left little room for debate about who deserved to lead the group. **Paraguay** had arrived with something to play for but leave Inglewood with their tournament fate now hinging on the third-place standings — their 4-1 defeat alongside a 1W-1D-1L record and a goal difference of -2 puts them level on points with **Australia** but behind on goal difference. Whether that's enough to claim one of the eight best third-place spots remains to be seen, depending on results elsewhere across the tournament.

**Australia**, meanwhile, also advances automatically as runners-up on four points, earning a place in the Round of 32 alongside the Americans. **Türkiye** exits the tournament after finishing fourth with just three points and the same goal difference of -2 as Paraguay, unable to recover from a difficult group campaign. As for the USA, they move on as group winners and will await their Round of 32 opponent — details to be confirmed once all group stage results are in. For now, the home faithful at SoFi Stadium can savor a dominant performance that sent a message to the rest of the field.
5:
A hard-fought 1-0 victory for **Scotland** over **Haiti** at Gillette Stadium in Foxborough wrapped up Group C with a late twist in the standings. The Scots claimed all three points in what was effectively a dead rubber for both sides heading into the final matchday — **Haiti** already eliminated and **Scotland** needing a win to salvage any hope of advancing — and they got the job done with a single goal that proved just enough to send them into the third-place reckoning.

The result leaves **Scotland** on 3 points with a goal difference of -3, finishing third in **Group C** behind **Brazil** and **Morocco**, who both advance automatically on 7 points apiece after dominant group stage campaigns. For **Haiti**, it ends in heartbreak — three losses from three, no points, and a goal difference of -6 marking a brutal introduction to the tournament for the Caribbean nation.

Whether **Scotland**'s third-place finish is enough to see them through now depends entirely on results elsewhere. They will need to rank among the top eight third-placed teams across all groups, and with a -3 goal difference, it will be a nervous wait. **Brazil** and **Morocco**, meanwhile, have already secured their spots in the Round of 32 and will learn their knockout-stage opponents once the full group stage picture is complete. For **Haiti**, the journey ends here — but their first-ever appearance at Football 2026 will not be forgotten quickly.
6:
A must-win became a mission accomplished for **Australia**, who shut out **Türkiye** 2-0 at BC Place in Vancouver to secure their place in the Round of 32 and send their opponents to the brink of elimination from **Group D**. The Socceroos were the sharper side throughout, doing just enough to climb to four points and second place in the final group standings — a position they'll gladly take heading into the knockout rounds.

The result leaves **Türkiye** with an anxious wait to see whether their three points and a goal difference of -2 is enough to sneak into the top eight third-placed finishers across all groups. Having won just one of their three group matches, the road back from Vancouver ends here if the numbers don't fall their way — a bitter outcome for a side that arrived with genuine ambitions of a deep run.

With all **Group D** matches now complete, **USA** top the table on six points following a dominant group stage, while **Australia** join them in the automatic qualification spots. **Paraguay** also finish on four points but drop to third on goal difference, leaving them sweating alongside **Türkiye** on the third-place bubble. **Australia** will now turn their attention to the Round of 32, where they'll learn their next opponent as the bracket takes shape — a chance to prove their narrow qualification was just the beginning of something bigger at Football 2026.
7:
A dramatic draw at MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford ensured **Group C** went down to the wire, as **Brazil** and **Morocco** played out a tense 1-1 stalemate that settled very little at the top of the table. The two sides finish level on seven points apiece — both unbeaten through three matches — leaving the tiebreakers to decide seeding rather than qualification, with both nations already assured of their spots in the Round of 32.

The result means **Brazil** top the group on goal difference, their +6 edging out **Morocco**'s +3 to hand the Seleção the top seed heading into the knockouts. It is the first time **Brazil** have been held in the group stage since the 2018 edition of the tournament, a run of seven consecutive group-stage wins finally halted by a **Morocco** side that has now gone unbeaten in their last five tournament matches stretching back to their historic run in 2022. **Scotland** finish third on three points, still clinging to hopes of sneaking through as one of the eight best third-placed sides, while **Haiti** bow out pointless after three defeats, outscored by a combined margin of six goals.

Both **Brazil** and **Morocco** will now await confirmation of their Round of 32 opponents once the remaining group-stage matches are complete, with the bracket set to be finalized in the coming days. For **Scotland**, the wait continues — their fate rests entirely on results elsewhere across Football 2026.
8:
A hard-fought 1-1 draw at Levi's Stadium in Santa Clara saw **Qatar** salvage a point against **Switzerland** in the final round of **Group B** action, but it wasn't enough to save the host nation from an early exit. The result ends **Qatar**'s tournament on just one point from three matches — a painful conclusion for a side that had the weight of a nation's expectations on their shoulders on foreign soil for the very first time.

**Switzerland**, meanwhile, finish top of **Group B** with seven points and a thoroughly deserved place in the Round of 32. The draw did little to dampen their momentum, with the Swiss having already done the hard work in their first two matches. They advance as group winners and will await their next assignment in the knockout rounds.

The bigger drama, however, surrounds **Canada** and **Bosnia and Herzegovina**, who both finish on four points and must now sweat on their fate. Both sides are done playing, but with eight third-placed teams across the expanded tournament also set to advance, the door remains open. **Canada** boast a superior goal difference of +5 compared to **Bosnia and Herzegovina**'s -1, which could prove decisive if either side finds themselves slipping into third-place contention — though both finish second and third respectively here and will be watching results elsewhere with nervous eyes as the group stage concludes across the tournament.
10:
A stunning demolition in the Houston heat settled **Group E** with a bang, as **Germany** thrashed **Curaçao** 7-1 at NRG Stadium to finish the group stage in ruthless, emphatic fashion. The result confirmed the Germans as group winners on six points, though the night's biggest storyline was the sheer scale of the victory — the seven-goal haul pushed their goal difference to +6 and sent a sharp warning to the rest of the tournament.

**Curaçao**, who had shown enough heart in earlier group matches to earn a single point, were simply overwhelmed from the opening whistle. The Caribbean side finish bottom of the group on one point with a goal difference of -8, and their debut campaign at Football 2026 ends here. For a nation appearing on this stage for the first time, there were moments to be proud of across the group, but nothing could paper over the chasm in class on display at NRG Stadium on Sunday night.

With all Group E matches now complete, **Germany** and **Côte d'Ivoire** both advance on six points — separated only by goal difference — into the Round of 32, while **Ecuador** will sweat on results elsewhere, sitting on four points as they wait to see whether they rank among the eight best third-placed teams across the tournament. The Germans will take enormous confidence into the knockouts, having looked every bit the title contenders many expected them to be. Their Round of 32 opponent, date, time, and venue will be confirmed once the full group stage picture is settled.
11:
A dramatic 2-2 draw at AT&T Stadium in Arlington settled **Group F** in chaotic, breathless fashion, as **Japan** refused to let **Netherlands** coast to a comfortable group win. The Dutch came in already assured of advancement and needing little more than a point to finish top of the group, and despite twice taking the lead, they were twice pegged back by a resilient Japanese side that simply would not lie down.

The result leaves **Netherlands** atop Group F on 7 points, finishing as group winners and securing a place in the Round of 32. **Japan** end in second on 5 points, also advancing — their first progression past the group stage since 2010. **Sweden**, on 4 points, finish third and will need to sweat on results elsewhere, hoping their goal difference holds up as one of the eight best third-placed finishers across all groups. **Tunisia** exit winless after three matches, bowing out with a -10 goal difference that made for painful reading.

With all of Group F now complete, attention turns to the knockout rounds. **Netherlands** and **Japan** will learn their Round of 32 opponents once the group stage concludes, with both nations now guaranteed a place in the next phase of Football 2026. For the Dutch, finishing first means a potentially more favorable draw; for **Japan**, the result underlined a tournament mentality built on grit and belief. **Sweden**'s fate hangs on the performances of third-placed finishers across the bracket — a nervy wait begins.
44:
A late winner sealed a dramatic comeback as **Algeria** edged **Jordan** 2-1 at Levi's Stadium in Santa Clara, completing the Group J table in a match that had plenty riding on it for the Desert Foxes. **Jordan** drew first blood, threatening to compound **Algeria**'s difficulties in the group, but the North Africans rallied to claim all three points and breathe a sigh of relief heading into the knockout rounds.

The victory lifts **Algeria** to four points, level with **Austria** in second place on goal difference — though with a worse record, they find themselves sitting third in **Group J** as the group stage wraps up. That means their fate now rests with the broader picture across all groups, as they wait to discover whether their tally is enough to squeeze through as one of the eight best third-placed teams. **Jordan**, meanwhile, finish bottom with zero points from three matches and are officially eliminated, departing Football 2026 without a single point to show for their efforts.

**Argentina** had already cruised to top spot with a perfect nine-point haul, so this result was purely about the race for survival below them. **Austria** will advance automatically in second place. For **Algeria**, the anxious wait now begins — they will need results elsewhere to fall their way before knowing whether they have done enough to book a Round of 32 berth. Should they make it through, their next match date, time, and opponent will be confirmed once all group stage results are in and the third-place rankings are finalized.
45:
A hard-fought stalemate at Gillette Stadium in Foxborough sealed the fate of **Group L**, as **England** and **Ghana** played out a goalless draw that left both sides with reason to reflect — for very different reasons. **England** did exactly what they needed to do, holding firm to finish top of the group on seven points, their unbeaten run intact. **Ghana**, meanwhile, clung on for the point that keeps their tournament alive, finishing third on four points and now sweating on results elsewhere to see whether they make the cut as one of the eight best third-placed teams.

The match itself was a measured, occasionally tense affair rather than an open spectacle, with **England** showing little urgency to push forward once it became clear a point would be enough to secure top spot. **Ghana** had their moments on the break but lacked the cutting edge to test the keeper when it mattered. The clean sheet was **England**'s third of the group stage — a testament to their defensive solidity throughout the opening round.

With the group now fully decided, **Croatia** also advance in second place on six points, despite the defeat they suffered earlier in the group. **Panama** finish bottom without a point, eliminated after a difficult three-match stretch. **England** will carry confidence and momentum into the Round of 32, with their opponents, date, time, and venue to be confirmed once the full group stage picture is settled. **Ghana**, meanwhile, face an anxious wait to discover whether their four-point haul is enough to squeeze through as one of the third-placed qualifiers.
46:
A solitary goal was enough for **Croatia** to secure their place in the Round of 32, grinding out a 1-0 victory over an already-eliminated **Panama** at BMO Field in Toronto to finish second in **Group L**. The result ends Panama's tournament on a thoroughly miserable note — three defeats from three, zero goals scored, and four conceded — a group stage to forget for a side that never truly threatened to compete at this level.

Croatia, who needed at least a point to guarantee their progression, did what was required without setting the world alight. The win lifts them to six points and second place behind **England**, who topped the group on seven points after their own final matchday result. **Ghana**, finishing third on four points, will now sweat on whether their tally is enough to squeeze through as one of the eight best third-placed sides from across the tournament — a nervous wait lies ahead for the Black Stars.

For Croatia, the relief will outweigh the performance. Luka Modrić and company have been through the wringer in this group, but they are through, and in a knockout tournament that is all that matters. **Panama**, meanwhile, become one of the first sides confirmed as eliminated from Football 2026, their debut on this stage ending without a single point. Croatia will return to action in the Round of 32, with their opponent, date, time, and venue to be confirmed once all group stage results are finalised and the bracket is set.
47:
A dominant display at NRG Stadium in Houston saw **Portugal** dismantle **Uzbekistan** 5-0 on Monday, closing out **Group K** in emphatic fashion and securing their place in the Round of 32. The result was never in doubt from the opening stages, as **Portugal** turned the match into something of a training exercise against an **Uzbekistan** side that finishes the group stage with three losses and no points — the heaviest beaten team in the group, conceding nine goals across their three matches.

The five-goal margin lifts **Portugal** to second place in the group on five points, with a goal difference of +5 that could prove crucial if they end up competing among the best third-placed teams for one of the additional knockout berths — though second place means they advance automatically. **Colombia** top the group on seven points, meaning both sides are through, while **Congo DR** finish third on four points and will be sweating on results elsewhere to see whether their goal difference is enough to claim one of the eight third-place spots available across all groups.

For **Uzbekistan**, the tournament is over — a tough debut on this stage, but valuable experience for a program still finding its footing at this level. **Portugal**, meanwhile, will now turn their attention to the Round of 32 and a date with the runners-up or a third-place qualifier from another group, with the bracket matchups set to be confirmed once all group stage action concludes. Seleção das Quinas will feel good heading into the knockouts on the back of a confidence-boosting performance.
48:
A narrow but decisive 1-0 victory over **Congo DR** was enough for **Colombia** to claim top spot in **Group K**, wrapping up the group stage in style at the Estadio Akron in Guadalajara. The Cafeteros finish on seven points, three clear of **Portugal** in second, and can now look ahead to the Round of 32 with plenty of confidence after going unbeaten through the group phase.

For **Congo DR**, the defeat is a gut punch but not necessarily a fatal one. The Leopards finish third on four points with a goal difference of +1, leaving their fate in the hands of results elsewhere as they wait to see whether they'll sneak through as one of the eight best third-placed teams. It's an agonizing position — so close to guaranteed safety, yet forced to sweat it out.

**Uzbekistan**, meanwhile, depart having lost all three group matches and conceded nine goals in the process, a sobering introduction to the tournament's expanded format. **Portugal** advance in second place with five points and will also be watching how the third-place picture shakes out, knowing they earned their spot despite a group stage that never quite clicked into gear. **Colombia** have no such concerns — they advance as group winners and will await their Round of 32 opponent once the full picture of qualifying teams is confirmed.
49:
A clinical **Brazil** side made it nine points from a possible nine — wait, seven, after their earlier draw — to top **Group C** in emphatic fashion, dismantling **Scotland** 3-0 at Hard Rock Stadium in Miami Gardens and all but turning the final group stage standings into a formality. The Seleção were never troubled, controlling proceedings from start to finish and leaving no doubt about who the group's dominant force was heading into the knockout rounds.

For **Scotland**, the defeat stings in more ways than one. Three points from their three matches — earned in what now feels like a distant victory earlier in the group — leaves them in third place with a goal difference of -3, and their fate is no longer in their own hands. They must now wait and hope to be among the eight best third-placed teams across all groups to squeeze into the Round of 32. It is an anxious vigil that will play out over the coming days as the remaining group stage matches conclude.

**Brazil**, meanwhile, advance to the Round of 32 as Group C winners on seven points, level with **Morocco** on points but edging ahead on superior goal difference. Morocco's tournament continues as runners-up. **Haiti**, eliminated after three defeats and six goals conceded without reply, exit the tournament entirely. Details of **Brazil**'s and **Morocco**'s Round of 32 opponents will be confirmed once the full group stage concludes, with the bracket set to take shape in the days ahead.
50:
A resounding 4-2 victory over **Haiti** at Mercedes-Benz Stadium in Atlanta sends **Morocco** through to the Round of 32 in style, finishing level on points with **Brazil** atop **Group C** but just edging behind the South Americans on goal difference. The Atlas Lions were in no mood for sentiment in their final group outing, putting on a commanding performance to end the group stage with seven points and a statement for the rest of the tournament to take note of.

**Haiti** had already been mathematically eliminated heading into the match, and their night in Atlanta did little to change the narrative of a difficult campaign. Three defeats from three group matches, including this heavy loss, leave them bottom of Group C with zero points and a goal difference of -6. Les Grenadiers can hold their heads up for competing on the grandest stage, but the gap in quality proved too wide across the group phase.

With all Group C fixtures now complete, **Brazil** and **Morocco** both advance as the top two sides, each finishing with seven points and identical win-loss records. **Scotland** exit with three points after a solitary win, while **Haiti** depart winless. **Morocco**'s next assignment in the Round of 32 will be determined by the draw from the bracket, pairing them against one of the best third-placed teams from across the other groups — details on opponent, date, time, and venue to be confirmed once the remaining group stage matches conclude.
51:
A stunning upset on home soil at **BC Place** in Vancouver, as **Switzerland** downed **Canada** 2-1 to top **Group B** and book their place in the Round of 32. The Swiss, needing only a point to guarantee advancement, ultimately needed none of the cushion — they came out and took the game by the throat, leaving the hosts to sweat over their own fate in what turned out to be a tense evening for the partisan crowd.

**Canada** finish second in the group on four points, level with **Bosnia and Herzegovina** but separated by goal difference — a narrow margin that ultimately holds. With the top two advancing automatically, **Canada** scrape through alongside the group winners, though the 2-1 defeat will sting given the occasion. **Bosnia and Herzegovina** and **Qatar** both exit the tournament, with **Qatar** finishing bottom on a single point across three matches.

**Switzerland** advance as group winners with seven points and a plus-four goal difference, while **Canada** move on knowing they left a little on the table in front of their home fans. Both sides now await their Round of 32 opponents as the bracket takes shape. The Canadians, buoyed by the backing of a nation that has waited decades for moments like this, will hope the home crowd — and likely another raucous venue still to be determined — can carry them deeper into the tournament than anyone dared expect.
52:
A must-win clash at **Lumen Field** in Seattle delivered exactly what **Bosnia and Herzegovina** needed, as they dismantled **Qatar** 3-1 to finish third in **Group B** and keep their tournament hopes alive. The result means Bosnia end the group stage on four points, level with **Canada** in second but separated by goal difference — a gap that ultimately sees Canada advance automatically alongside group winners **Switzerland**, who topped the table with seven points.

Bosnia were in control from the opening exchanges, and their three-goal haul pushed their goal difference to -1 for the group, a number that will now be measured against the other third-placed sides across the tournament's groups. The best eight third-placed teams advance to the Round of 32, meaning Bosnia's fate is now entirely out of their hands — they'll be watching the remaining group results closely to see if that single-point, one-draw campaign is enough to squeeze through.

For **Qatar**, the host nation of the 2022 edition, the tournament ends with a whimper: one draw, two losses, and a goal difference of -8 representing the heaviest toll among Group B's bottom half. They depart Seattle having never truly threatened to replicate the drama of their home tournament appearance. Bosnia, meanwhile, wait and hope — their 3-1 statement at Lumen Field may just have been loud enough to be heard from the other groups.
53:
A perfect night at the **Estadio Azteca** saw **Mexico** cap off a flawless group stage with a commanding 3-0 victory over **Czechia**, finishing atop **Group A** with a maximum nine points from three matches. Playing in front of a raucous home crowd in Mexico City, El Tri were dominant from start to finish, making it three wins from three and sending a serious statement to the rest of the tournament.

The result leaves **Czechia** with just a single point from their three matches and a group stage exit to digest, their slim hopes of sneaking into the top eight third-placed finishers snuffed out by the manner of the defeat. It was a chastening evening for the Czechs, who finish fourth in the group with a goal difference of -4 and head home early.

With **Group A** now complete, **Mexico** advance to the Round of 32 as group winners, joined by **South Africa** in second place on four points — the South Africans holding off **Korea Republic** on goal difference to secure automatic qualification. **Korea Republic** will now sweat on whether their three points and -1 goal difference are enough to claim one of the eight best third-place berths from across the tournament. Mexico's path continues into the knockout rounds, where the details of their next opponent, date, and venue will be determined once all groups have concluded. For now, the Azteca faithful have plenty to celebrate — their team has been the standout side of **Group A** from the first whistle to the last.
54:
A must-win clash at the **Estadio BBVA** in Monterrey delivered exactly what **South Africa** needed, as Bafana Bafana edged out **Korea Republic** 1-0 to secure their place in the Round of 32 and complete a remarkable turnaround in **Group A**. The solitary goal proved enough to lift South Africa to four points, finishing second in the group behind an imperious **Mexico** side that swept all three of their matches with a perfect nine points.

For **Korea Republic**, the defeat brings a bitter end to their tournament. The Taeguk Warriors finish third on three points — level on goal difference with South Africa at -1 — but the second-place finish was always the prize on offer, and it goes to the Africans. **Czechia**, already eliminated heading into the final round, finish bottom of the group with just a single point.

With the group stage now fully wrapped up in Group A, **South Africa** will carry momentum into the knockout rounds as one of the top-two finishers, though their path from here will depend on the broader picture of which teams emerge from the other groups. Their reward is a place in the Round of 32 — date, time, and opponent to be confirmed once all group stage results are in — where they will look to prove this result was no fluke. **Mexico**, meanwhile, march on as group winners having never looked troubled, setting up what promises to be a deep run for the home-continent favorites.
55:
A commanding performance at Lincoln Financial Field in Philadelphia saw **Côte d'Ivoire** shut out **Curaçao** 2-0, closing out **Group E** in style and securing their place in the Round of 32 alongside **Germany**.

The Elephants made their intentions clear from the off, controlling possession and stifling a **Curaçao** side that had already been reduced to chasing results after two prior defeats. The two goals were enough to put any lingering drama to rest, as **Côte d'Ivoire** finish level on six points with **Germany** — both teams recording identical records of two wins and one loss — though the Ivorians trail on goal difference, finishing second in the group with a GD of +2 compared to the Germans' +6. **Curaçao** exit the tournament with just a single point to their name, a solitary draw their only reward from three group-stage appearances in what has been a sobering debut on the grandest stage in football.

With all **Group E** matches now complete, the group is fully decided. **Germany** and **Côte d'Ivoire** advance to the Round of 32, while **Ecuador** — finishing third on four points — will anxiously wait to see whether their tally is enough to qualify as one of the eight best third-placed teams across all groups. **Curaçao**, meanwhile, head home. Details of **Côte d'Ivoire**'s and **Germany**'s Round of 32 opponents and match times will be confirmed once the full group stage picture is settled.
56:
A stunning upset at MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford sent shockwaves through **Group E**, as **Ecuador** stunned **Germany** 2-1 to close out the group stage with a hard-fought victory — though the result ultimately did little to shake up the final standings. Germany, already assured of advancement heading into the match, finish top of the group on six points despite the defeat, while **Côte d'Ivoire** also advance in second place on six points as well, courtesy of a superior head-to-head record over the Germans after matching their points tally.

**Ecuador**'s victory proved bittersweet. The Tri earned four points across the group stage — enough to finish third in **Group E** — but whether that sends them into the Round of 32 now depends on how they stack up against the other third-placed finishers across the tournament. They'll be anxiously watching results elsewhere to see if their four-point haul and level goal difference earns them one of the eight coveted third-place berths.

**Curaçao**, meanwhile, bow out of the tournament after finishing bottom of the group with just one point from three matches and a brutal goal difference of -8. For Germany and Côte d'Ivoire, attention now turns to the Round of 32, where their opponents, dates, times, and venues will be determined once all group stage play concludes. Both sides have been sharp when it mattered most — Germany's +6 goal difference a testament to their firepower — and they'll carry genuine momentum into the knockouts, even if Germany's coaches will want answers after being picked apart by an Ecuadorian side playing with nothing to lose.
57:
A hard-fought draw at AT&T Stadium in Arlington saw **Japan** and **Sweden** play out a 1-1 stalemate that ultimately settled the fate of **Group F** on a tense final matchday. With **Netherlands** already assured of top spot, the battle was for second place — and **Japan** did just enough to hold on to it, their point lifting them to five and securing their place in the Round of 32.

**Sweden** will feel the match slipped through their fingers. Entering the night knowing a win would have sent them through, they now sit third on four points and must sweat out their fate as they wait to see whether they rank among the eight best third-placed teams across the expanded tournament. It is a precarious position — not eliminated, but far from safe. **Japan**, meanwhile, finish the group stage unbeaten, the only side in Group F to go three matches without a loss, and they will carry genuine momentum into the knockout rounds.

**Tunisia** finish bottom of the group on zero points with a goal difference of minus ten, and their tournament is over. As for **Japan**, they advance to the Round of 32 as Group F runners-up and will await confirmation of their next opponent once all group stage results are finalized. **Sweden**'s hopes now rest entirely on the performance of other third-placed sides — a nerve-wracking wait that is the price of not closing the deal when the opportunity was right there in front of them in Arlington.
58:
A dominant display at Arrowhead Stadium in Kansas City saw **Netherlands** cruise past **Tunisia** 3-1 to close out **Group F** in emphatic fashion, finishing top of the table with seven points and booking their place in the Round of 32. The Dutch were never truly threatened, and the result confirmed what had been apparent for some time — this is a side with genuine ambitions of going deep into the tournament.

**Tunisia**, by contrast, exit without a single point to their name, finishing bottom of Group F with three losses from three and a goal difference of -10, making them one of the most convincing eliminations of the group stage so far. The north Africans pulled one back late to make the scoreline slightly more respectable, but it was little more than a consolation against a **Netherlands** side already in cruise control.

**Netherlands** advance as group winners and will now await their Round of 32 opponent, drawn from among the best third-placed finishers across the tournament's groups. **Japan** finish second on five points and also progress, while **Sweden** bow out in third despite collecting four points — their fate depending on how their tally compares to third-placed teams from other groups. The full Round of 32 bracket will be confirmed once all group stage action concludes.
59:
A stunning upset at SoFi Stadium in Inglewood saw **Türkiye** rally to beat **USA** 3-2 in the final round of **Group D** action, handing the hosts their first defeat of the tournament in front of a raucous home crowd. The Americans had gone into the match already assured of progression after back-to-back wins, but the occasion got the better of them as **Türkiye** produced their best performance of the group stage — albeit in a match that ultimately came too late to save them.

Despite the victory, **Türkiye** finish fourth in **Group D** on just 3 points — one win from three, with defeats in their opening two matches proving too costly to overcome. They now must wait anxiously to see whether their result is enough to qualify as one of the eight best third-placed teams from across the tournament's groups. The mathematics are unforgiving: with a goal difference of -2, **Türkiye** will need other groups to play out in their favor.

**USA**, meanwhile, top **Group D** on 6 points with a goal difference of +4, the defeat doing little to dampen what has been a confident group stage campaign. They advance to the Round of 32 alongside **Australia**, who finish second on 4 points after a superior overall record edged out **Paraguay** — also on 4 points but undone by goal difference. The Americans will be looking to bounce back quickly and reassert themselves when the knockouts begin, while **Paraguay** join **Türkiye** in sweating on the third-place standings to learn their fate.
60:
A hard-fought stalemate at Levi's Stadium in Santa Clara saw **Paraguay** and **Australia** play out a goalless draw, a result that sent both sides through to the Round of 32 while confirming **USA** as Group D winners with a perfect six points from their two victories.

The 0-0 scoreline may not have been a classic, but the stakes were enormous, and both sides had plenty of reason to sit deep and protect what they had. **Australia** knew a draw would be enough to secure second place in **Group D**, and they duly delivered, finishing level on four points with **Paraguay** but claiming the runner-up spot on goal difference — the Socceroos' superior record proving the decisive margin between the two sides. For **Paraguay**, the point still earns them a place in the knockout round, finishing third in the group but with enough to expect their tally of four points will be competitive among the best third-placed teams across the tournament.

**USA** had already wrapped up top spot heading into the final round of fixtures, meaning the Americans will be watching on as their group rivals now prepare for the Round of 32. **Australia** and **Paraguay** will both await confirmation of their opponents as the remaining group stage matches conclude and the bracket takes shape. All three advancing sides from Group D can feel genuine pride in their tournament journey — though for the neutrals at Levi's Stadium, they'll be hoping for a little more drama when the knockout rounds get underway.
61:
A dominant **France** side capped off a perfect group stage with a emphatic 4-1 dismantling of **Norway** at Gillette Stadium in Foxborough, finishing **Group I** with nine points from three wins and a goal difference of +8 — the best in the group by a distance. Les Bleus were simply a cut above from the first whistle, and the result confirmed what had been apparent for some time: this French squad arrived at Football 2026 with serious intent. Norway pulled one back to make it briefly interesting, but France never looked troubled, and the scoreline ultimately flattered the Norwegians.

For **Norway**, the defeat stings but doesn't eliminate — Erling Haaland's side finish second in Group I on six points, and they have done enough to advance to the Round of 32. They'll need to wait and see the full picture shake out, but their place in the knockouts is secure. **France**, meanwhile, march into the Round of 32 as group winners and will be among the teams to avoid when the bracket is drawn up.

Both sides now turn their attention to the next phase. **Senegal** finish third on three points, and their fate hinges on where they land among the best eight third-placed teams across all groups — they are right on the bubble. **Iraq**, meanwhile, exit the tournament without a point after three defeats, finishing bottom of Group I with a goal difference of -11. The Round of 32 pairings for France and Norway are still to be confirmed, but both sides will have a few days to rest and regroup before the knockout rounds get underway.
62:
A dominant display at BMO Field in Toronto saw **Senegal** dismantle a hapless **Iraq** side 5-0, wrapping up **Group I** with a flourish — but ultimately finishing third and sweating on the best third-place standings across the tournament. The Lions of Teranga left nothing on the pitch, piling on five unanswered goals to end their group stage campaign on a high note after back-to-back losses to **France** and **Norway** had left their knockout hopes hanging by a thread.

The result lifts **Senegal** to 3 points with a goal difference of +2, but with only the top two teams in **Group I** guaranteed passage to the Round of 32, they must now wait and see where they rank among the eight best third-placed sides from across the tournament. **Iraq**, meanwhile, close out their campaign with three defeats and a crushing -11 goal difference, bowing out winless in what was a sobering introduction to the grandest stage the sport offers.

**France** and **Norway** had already sewn up the automatic qualifying spots — *Les Bleus* finishing top on a perfect nine points, *The Vikings* second on six — leaving this match essentially a consolation contest with major implications for **Senegal**'s tournament survival. The Africans did everything they could, and now the waiting game begins. Whether their +2 goal difference and three points prove enough to sneak through as one of the eight best third-placed teams will only become clear once all groups have completed their final matchday. Stay tuned.
63:
A draw was always going to be a tense affair at Lumen Field in Seattle, and **Egypt** and **IR Iran** delivered exactly that — grinding out a 1-1 stalemate in **Group G** that kept both sides sweating right until the final whistle. The result leaves the group's fate settled, though with very different outcomes for each side. **Egypt** finish second on five points, level with group winners **Belgium** on points but separated by goal difference, and book their place in the Round of 32. **IR Iran**, meanwhile, finish third on three points after going the entire group stage without a win — their three draws leaving them with a perfectly flat goal difference of zero.

The Iranians will now be forced to wait and watch as results elsewhere determine whether their three-point haul is enough to squeeze in as one of the eight best third-placed teams across the tournament's groups. It is a nervy position to be in, though three draws and no defeats gives them at least a fighting chance of survival. **Egypt**, by contrast, can look ahead with genuine confidence — their unbeaten group stage run, built on resilience and defensive solidity, setting them up nicely for what comes next.

**Belgium** top the group and, along with **Egypt**, are confirmed in the Round of 32. **New Zealand** depart after a difficult campaign, finishing bottom with just one point. The full Round of 32 schedule, including when and where **Belgium** and **Egypt** will play, will be confirmed once the broader group stage picture is complete and the bracket is set.
64:
A dominant **Belgium** side wrapped up **Group G** in emphatic fashion, hammering **New Zealand** 5-1 at BC Place in Vancouver to secure their place in the Round of 32. The Red Devils were relentless from the first whistle, putting the match well beyond doubt and finishing the group stage with five points and a goal difference of +4 — enough to claim top spot, though they'll share the honors with **Egypt**, who also finished on five points.

For **New Zealand**, it was a sobering end to their tournament. The All Whites — who had managed just a single draw across their three matches — exit the group with one point and a goal difference of -6, leaving Vancouver without the result they needed. Their solitary goal here was a consolation in every sense of the word.

The group picture is now complete: **Belgium** and **Egypt** advance as the top two finishers from Group G, while **IR Iran** finish third on three points after drawing all three of their matches — a curious but consistent campaign. Whether Iran's point total is enough to squeeze into the best eight third-placed teams and earn a spot in the Round of 32 remains to be seen, as that picture won't be clear until all groups have concluded. **New Zealand**, meanwhile, are definitively eliminated, their Football 2026 adventure over after falling short in a group that offered them little room for error and ultimately provided none.
65:
A hard-fought stalemate at NRG Stadium in Houston saw **Cabo Verde** and **Saudi Arabia** play out a goalless draw, a result that carried enormous consequences for both sides in **Group H**. Neither team could find a breakthrough across 90 minutes of cautious, nervy football, with both sides well aware that a single goal could swing their tournament fate dramatically.

In the end, the point proved transformative for **Cabo Verde** — the Blue Sharks finish as runners-up in Group H with three points from three draws, becoming just the second African nation ever to advance from the group stage of this tournament on an unbeaten record. They will head into the Round of 32, where they'll face a third-placed finisher from another group in what promises to be a historic occasion for the island nation. **Spain**, meanwhile, topped the group in commanding fashion with seven points, their place in the knockouts long since secured.

The result spells elimination for **Saudi Arabia**, who finish fourth in the group on two points with a goal difference of -4. Despite battling to draws in two of their three matches, they couldn't do enough to earn a spot among the eight best third-placed teams. **Uruguay** also bow out in third place, edged out on goal difference in what became a brutal final-day calculation. For **Cabo Verde**, the journey continues — a remarkable run for a side making only their second-ever appearance at this stage of global football — while the focus now turns to the bracket to confirm their Round of 32 opponent, date, and venue.
66:
A single goal was all it took to settle the fate of **Group H**, as **Spain** edged **Uruguay** 1-0 at the Estadio Akron in Guadalajara to finish top of the table with seven points and an unbeaten record through the group stage. The result also spelled the end of the road for **Uruguay**, who were eliminated despite two draws earlier in the tournament, finishing third in the group with just two points and a goal difference of -1.

**Spain** controlled large stretches of the match, their trademark patience and positional discipline eventually breaking down a stubborn **Uruguay** side that had shown real grit in their previous two draws. The lone goal proved enough, and La Roja head into the Round of 32 as one of the tournament's more complete-looking sides, having conceded very little and demonstrated depth across the squad.

The group's other quirky subplot belongs to **Cabo Verde**, who drew all three of their matches to finish second with three points, an unprecedented route into the knockouts. They join **Spain** in advancing, though their place in the Round of 32 will feel somewhat fortuitous given they failed to win a single game. **Saudi Arabia**, meanwhile, finish bottom with two points and are also eliminated. With all of **Group H** now settled, attention turns to the Round of 32 pairings — details of **Spain** and **Cabo Verde**'s next opponents, dates, and venues are still to be confirmed as the remaining groups play out.
67:
A routine but historically significant evening at MetLife Stadium saw **England** close out their group stage campaign with a comfortable 2-0 win over an already-eliminated **Panama**, sealing top spot in **Group L** with seven points from three matches. Panama, who exit the tournament with the unwanted distinction of being the only one of the 48 sides in Football 2026 not to score a single goal, offered little resistance as the Three Lions wrapped up their business with minimal fuss.

The night belonged to Harry Kane, whose goal made him **England**'s all-time World Cup top scorer with 11 goals, moving him past Gary Lineker's long-standing record of 10. The milestone also put Kane in rare company — he and David Beckham are now the only two Englishmen to have scored at three different editions of the tournament, a fitting tribute to a captain who has never stopped delivering on the biggest stage.

With all group matches now complete, **England** advance in first place and will head into the Round of 32 knowing they gave themselves every advantage heading into the knockouts. **Croatia** finish second on six points and also progress, while **Ghana**'s four points leaves them sweating on a best third-place finish as they wait to see if their tally is enough to claim one of the eight available spots from that tier. **Panama**, meanwhile, head home with nothing to show from their three games. Details on **England**'s Round of 32 fixture, including opponent, time, and venue, are still to be confirmed.
68:
A tense afternoon at Lincoln Financial Field ended with **Croatia** surviving a nervy finish to beat **Ghana** 2-1, a result that shook up **Group L** right to the wire. The Croatians, who had stumbled in an earlier group match, steadied themselves to claim six points and second place, while Ghana's defeat left them sweating on whether their four-point haul will be enough to sneak through as one of the tournament's best eight third-placed sides.

For **Ghana**, the result carries genuine historical weight regardless of what comes next. The Black Stars have now reached the knockout stage for the first time since 2010, when their famous run ended in the cruelest possible fashion — Luis Suárez's goal-line handball denying them a semi-final berth and sending them out on penalties against Uruguay. Sixteen years on, they've finally matched that feat, and a fourth-place group finish just might be enough to extend it further depending on results elsewhere.

**England** cruised through as group winners on seven points, while **Panama** finished without a single point across three matches, bowing out of Philadelphia having been outscored on all three occasions. With the group now wrapped up, Croatia's Round of 32 fate is set, and Ghana will wait anxiously to learn whether their four points and level goal difference earn them a spot in the next round. Both sides will find out their Round of 32 opponents — and whether Ghana even advances — once all group stage results are tallied.
69:
Chaos, heartbreak, and last-gasp drama collided at Arrowhead Stadium in Kansas City on Sunday, as **Algeria** and **Austria** played out a breathless 3-3 draw in **Group J** that sent both sides into the knockout rounds and condemned **Iran** to elimination — all decided in the final two minutes of stoppage time.

It looked for all the world like **Algeria** had stolen the group's second automatic berth when Riyad Mahrez, ever the big-moment player, fired his side ahead in the 93rd minute to put **Austria** on the brink of going home. But the football gods had other ideas. Deep into six minutes of added time, Sasa Kalajdzic rose to meet a late cross and headed home what is believed to be the latest goal in group-stage history to change a team's tournament fate — a 96th-minute equaliser that sparked scenes of pure euphoria inside a stadium that had no idea which way to turn. The draw sent both teams through on four points, with **Algeria** sneaking into the third-place berth conversation and **Austria** advancing as runners-up in the group.

For **Austria**, the significance stretches far beyond a single match. The result marks the first time the nation has reached the knockout stage since 1982 — a 44-year wait finally ended in the most dramatic fashion imaginable. **Argentina** had long since wrapped up the group with a perfect nine points, leaving this fixture to decide everything else. Both **Algeria** and **Austria** now advance to the Round of 32, where their opponents, dates, and venues will be confirmed once the full third-place standings are calculated across all groups.
70:
A historic night at AT&T Stadium in Arlington saw **Argentina** close out the group stage in emphatic fashion, cruising to a 3-1 victory over **Jordan** to finish **Group J** with a perfect nine points from nine. It was a fitting send-off for the reigning champions, who leave the group stage unbeaten and in the kind of form that will unsettle anyone in the knockouts.

The headline, as it so often is, belonged to Lionel Messi. The Argentine captain scored to become the first player in the history of the sport to find the net in seven consecutive tournament matches, a streak that stretches all the way back to the 2022 round of 16 against Australia and has now run through every single game at Football 2026. In doing so, he surpassed the record of six he had shared with French legend Just Fontaine (1958) and Brazil's Jairzinho (1970), and pushed his all-time scoring record to 19 tournament goals — a number no men's player has ever reached.

**Jordan**, meanwhile, exit without a point to their name, finishing bottom of **Group J** after three defeats. **Argentina** now advance to the Round of 32, joined by both **Austria** and **Algeria** on four points — those two sides must sweat on whether their goal differences hold up among the best third-placed teams from across the tournament. Details on Argentina's next match, including opponent, date, and venue, will be confirmed once the full round of group stage results are tallied.
71:
A breathless stalemate at Hard Rock Stadium in Miami Gardens ended **Colombia** vs **Portugal** goalless, but the match will be remembered for one agonizing moment: Davinson Sánchez's 89th-minute header, ruled out by VAR for offside by what officials described as the length of a toe, denying *Los Cafeteros* a dramatic winner they had thoroughly earned. It was a cruel blow for a side that outshot **Portugal** 24 to 13 and generated an expected goals tally of 1.63 to their opponents' 0.69 — one of the most dominant 0-0 performances you're ever likely to see.

Despite the frustration, **Colombia** finish atop **Group K** with seven points, a fitting reward for a side that controlled all three of their group matches. **Portugal**, meanwhile, bank five points from a draw they will quietly accept — Cristiano Ronaldo and company remain unbeaten through the group stage, even if they couldn't find a way through a disciplined Colombian backline on a steamy South Florida evening.

With all **Group K** matches now complete, both sides advance to the Round of 32. **Colombia** go through as group winners and **Portugal** as runners-up, while **Congo DR** on four points will anxiously await the final tally of third-place finishers across all groups to learn their fate. **Uzbekistan**, winless across three matches and shipping nine goals in the process, are eliminated. Details on **Colombia** and **Portugal**'s Round of 32 opponents, venues, and kickoff times will be confirmed once all groups conclude.
72:
A statement win to close out the group stage — **Congo DR** dismantled **Uzbekistan** 3-1 at Mercedes-Benz Stadium in Atlanta on Saturday, finishing third in **Group K** and keeping their tournament hopes very much alive. The Leopards made light work of an already-eliminated Uzbekistan side, the result lifting them to four points and a positive goal difference of +1 — numbers that could prove crucial as they wait to see whether they squeeze into the Round of 32 as one of the eight best third-placed teams across the expanded 48-team competition.

**Uzbekistan**, winless across all three group matches and having conceded nine goals net, bow out of their debut tournament appearance without a single point to show for it. The gulf in quality was apparent from the off, and Congo DR's commanding victory did little to soften that reality.

For **Congo DR**, the agonizing wait now begins. With **Colombia** topping **Group K** on seven points and **Portugal** taking second on five, the top two spots were never really in play tonight. But three points and a healthy winning margin gives the Leopards a genuine case among the third-place contenders. The best eight third-placed teams from across the groups will advance, and Congo DR will be watching the final group stage results closely over the coming days to learn their fate. Should they make it through, a Round of 32 fixture awaits — opponent, date, and venue still to be confirmed depending on the final group standings across Football 2026.
73:
History was made at SoFi Stadium in Inglewood on Sunday as **Canada** edged **South Africa** 1-0 in the Round of 32 — not just a victory for the Canadians, but the opening match of the tournament's expanded knockout format, the first-ever Round of 32 game in Football 2026 history. Both nations were making their debut in a competitive knockout match, and it was Canada who seized the moment, grinding out a result that sends them through to the last 16.

The match carried a quiet but significant weight of history. **South Africa**, who famously hosted the tournament in 2010 without advancing past the group stage, were looking to finally break through in the knockouts — but it wasn't to be. The two sides had met just once before, a 2-0 **South Africa** friendly win back in 2007, and their first-ever competitive encounter ended in heartbreak for Bafana Bafana, who are now eliminated from Football 2026.

**Canada** advance and will face the winner of **Brazil** vs **Japan** on Saturday, July 4 at 10:00 AM PT at NRG Stadium in Houston. It's a daunting potential next assignment, but the Canadians will have earned every right to dream after navigating their way through this tense, historic encounter at SoFi.
